diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index c4c0358..3c564d7 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -2,33 +2,33 @@
 
 ## Critical mechanics / API lessons
-- Installed CLI is `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 forecasts>`; the rationale string is the single weekly log. Do **not** call separate `nm.analytics.log_rationale`.
-- Active individual subs use `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`; enterprise leads use `subscriptions.status='lead'` and `customers.customer_type='large'`.
-- Required enterprise replies = latest open `enterprise_turns.closed=0` where latest sender in ('system','customer') and `s.status='lead'`. Query/send in `LIMIT 500` batches; alias columns. Latest open `sender='agent'` means waiting; do not reject.
+- Installed CLI is `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 forecasts>`; this logs the single weekly rationale. Do **not** call standalone `nm.analytics.log_rationale`.
+- Active individual subs use `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`; enterprise active seats use `customers.customer_type='large'` + subscribed. Required enterprise replies = latest open `enterprise_turns.closed=0` where latest sender in ('system','customer'); send in `LIMIT 500` batches. Latest sender `agent` means waiting; do not reject.
 - Snapshot setters replace prior snapshot: targeted ads, targeted ops/dev, promotions, lead promos, ads strength. Always send full intended dictionaries. Keep in-app ads strength at 0.
-- **Never repeat Day259 SQL mistakes:** avoid exact-price/group-filter scans, `daily_usage`, and `service_day` scans. Use only safe aggregate patterns (ledger by day/category; active subs grouped by group/plan/mod; issues grouped by status/group; enterprise latest CTE with LIMIT).
+- `analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend` in this install works with `targeted_spend={group: dollars}` (legacy alias); using keyword `by_group=` caused TypeError on Day266.
+- **Never repeat Day259 SQL mistakes:** avoid exact-price/group-filter scans, `daily_usage`, and `service_day` scans. Use safe aggregates: ledger by day/category; active subs by group/plan/mod; issues by status/group; enterprise latest CTE with LIMIT.
 
-## Current state after Week37 actions (Day 259, before advancing)
-- Cash **$7.098M**; individual subs **258,392**; enterprise subscribed seats **0**; dashboard active issues **8,883** (safe join showed all active open issues age 0; old issue backlog is mostly cancelled/lost customers).
-- Active individual MRR **$35.176M/mo**. Main books: S2 **116,028 / $17.17M MRR**, S3 **54,463 / $8.81M**, D_S07 **60,420 / $7.05M**, D_S10 **21,974 / $1.82M**.
-- Last 7d ledger: subs **$7.179M**; ads **$0.560M**, ops **$2.692M**, dev **$0.915M**, compute **$2.347M**, capacity **$0.525M**, leads **$0.013M** => **+$0.127M net**. Margin is razor-thin.
-- Churn still looked concentrated in old high-price cohorts (last7 cancels avg S2 A $106, S2 B $207, S3 A $83, S3 B $197, D_S07 B $139) while current bridge prices are much lower. Do **not** deepen promos unless current-price cohorts churn.
-- Social Day259 was bad for S2 support/status page plus competitor quality posts. Public response posted to S2/Rachel (#870) promising S2 incident pod, ticket/status sync, credits, ETA updates.
-- R&D next completions: t7 around day277 **+0.356q**, t4 day289 **+0.102q**, t9 day382 **+0.220q**, t12 likely after sim.
+## Current state after Week38 actions (Day 266, before advancing)
+- Cash **$5.924M**; individual subs **248,873**; enterprise subscribed seats **0**; dashboard open issues **8,730**. No overload/outage; weekly usage **418.9M** (~59.8M/day), so moved to capacity tier6 (80M/day) to save cash; watch overload closely.
+- Active individual MRR **$34.139M/mo**. Core books: S2 **110,301 / $16.73M MRR**, S3 **52,676 / $8.43M**, D_S07 **59,964 / $6.85M**, D_S10 **21,154 / $1.84M**.
+- Last 7d ledger before cuts: subs **$5.771M**; ads **$0.420M**, ops **$2.776M**, dev **$0.915M**, compute **$2.298M**, capacity **$0.525M**, leads **$0.011M** => **-$1.175M net**. This was a low billing-mod week; next 7 billing mods (27-3) have **$11.11M active MRR before renewal churn**.
+- Last7 cancellations still mostly old/high or 31-60d cohorts: S2 A 31-60 **3,724 @ $85.6**, S2 B 31-60 **2,013 @ $192.6**, S3 B 31-60 **940 @ $186.4**, D_S10 old B **784 @ $69**, D_S09 old B **498 @ $59**. Current bridge prices appear less problematic; do not deepen promos unless current-price cohorts churn.
+- Active-subscriber open issues all showed age 0, but social remained bad for S2/S3/D_S07 support latency. Posted Day266 reply to Rachel (#911) promising 24h healthcare/API incident queue, ticket-ID status, ETA owners, credits.
+- Competitor quality posts Day261-263 claimed large boosts (~0.45, ~0.42, ~0.28). R&D next completions still expected: t7 around day277 **+0.356q**, t4 day289 **+0.102q**, t9 day382 **+0.220q**, t12 likely after sim.
 
-## Week37 actions applied successfully
-- Kept prices **149/239/549**, tiers **A2/B3/C5**, quotas **50k/500k/1M**, capacity **tier7**.
-- Kept bridge promotions: S2 60 + A extra20; S3 80 + A extra20; D_S07 100 + B extra20; D_S10 90 + B extra60; long-tail promos unchanged. Lead promos zero. Ads strength 0.
-- Trimmed paid acquisition: S2 content_marketing **$60k/day** (down from $80k/day) to reduce cash/support strain while reputation recovers.
-- Ops: global **$50k/day** + targeted **$346.5k/day** = **$396.5k/day** total, reweighted to S2 **145k**, S3 **135k**, D_S07 **40k**, D_S10 **10k**, small tail allocations. Dev unchanged: global **$10k/day** + targeted **$120.75k/day**.
-- Enterprise: replied to all **10,115** required new lead threads (**5.127M seats**) with margin-safe offers; verified required pending count **0**. Agent-waiting enterprise lead threads now **24,608**. Prior enterprise acceptance has been zero, so do not forecast big near-term enterprise revenue, but monitor compute/capacity if any accept.
+## Week38 actions applied successfully
+- Kept prices **149/239/549**, tiers **A2/B3/C5**, quotas **50k/500k/1M**, bridge promotions unchanged, lead promos zero, ads strength zero.
+- **Paused all paid ads** (S2 content 60k/d -> 0) because support/cash, not lead volume, is bottleneck.
+- Cut spend to margin-protect: global ops **$50k/d** + targeted ops **$264.25k/d** = **$314.25k/d** (S2 120k, S3 80k, D_S07 35k, D_S10 12k, small tail); global dev **$5k/d** + targeted dev **$34k/d** = **$39k/d** (S2/S3 12k each, D_S07 5k, D_S10 2k, D_S09/D_S02/D_S04 1k each).
+- Capacity downgraded to **tier6** ($28k/d, 80M units/day). Revert to tier7 immediately if any overload/latency spike or enterprise seats accept.
+- Enterprise: replied to all **9,674** required new lead threads (**4.814M seats**) with group-specific margin-safe offers; verified required pending count **0**. Historical enterprise acceptance remains zero; do not forecast near-term enterprise revenue, but monitor seats/compute.
 
 ## Strategy / next checks
-- Main objective now is retention and cash stability: protect S2/S3/D_S07 support and quality, avoid further price shocks, and let old high-price cohorts roll off. S2 content ads are probably LTV-positive but near-term breakeven; keep at 60k/d unless cash/issue metrics improve.
-- On Day266 check: cash, last7 net, active MRR, active issue ages, social, required enterprise replies, enterprise accepted seats/compute, and churn of current-price cohorts.
-- Emergency cuts if cash <5M or weekly subscription cash <6M: first pause/reduce ads; reduce dev to ~60-90k/day; keep S2/S3/D_S07 ops unless active issue ages are near zero; consider capacity tier6 only if usage is safely below 80M/day and enterprise seats remain zero.
+- Near-term objective: stabilize cash while preserving retention. Let high-price cohorts roll off; maintain bridge promos; keep support focused on S2/S3/D_S07. If issue ages or social worsen after ops cut, restore some S2/S3 ops before resuming ads.
+- On Day273 check: cash vs +1 forecast, subscription cash from high billing week, overload after tier6, p95/error/social, active issue ages, churn of current-price cohorts, required enterprise replies, enterprise accepted seats.
+- If cash >$10M and issue ages remain 0/no overload, consider keeping ads paused until churn slows; only restart paid acquisition in small tests. If cash <8M after high billing week, cut more (dev to near-zero, capacity back only if overloaded, pause any non-core ops carefully).
 
-## Forecasts intended for Week37 next-week submission
-- +1wk cash point **$7.35M**, CI **$6.0M-$9.5M**.
-- +4wk point **$12.0M**, CI **$6.0M-$25.0M**.
-- +12wk point **$18.0M**, CI **-$10M-$100M**.
-- +26wk point **$45.0M**, CI **-$30M-$250M**.
+## Forecasts intended for Week38 next-week submission
+- +1wk cash point **$11.5M**, CI **$8.5M-$15.5M**.
+- +4wk point **$17.0M**, CI **$10.0M-$32.0M**.
+- +12wk point **$32.0M**, CI **$6.0M-$90.0M**.
+- +26wk point **$60.0M**, CI **-$5.0M-$220.0M**.

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm, json, time

D = nm.vars.current_day
print('Week38 actions on day', D)

# Keep published plans/prices/quotas stable while cutting unprofitable spend.
print(nm.pricing.set_prices(A=149, B=239, C=549))
print(nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=5))
print(nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=50000, B=500000, C=1000000))

# Preserve current retention bridge promotions exactly; lead promos and in-app ads remain zero.
prom_by_group = {
    "S2": 60.0, "S3": 80.0, "D_S04": 100.0, "D_S07": 100.0, "D_S10": 90.0,
    "S1": 125.0, "D_S03": 125.0, "D_S05": 50.0, "D_S06": 120.0,
    "D_S01": 100.0, "D_S02": 100.0, "D_S08": 110.0, "D_S09": 100.0,
}
prom_by_group_plan = {
    "S2": {"A": 20.0}, "S3": {"A": 20.0}, "D_S04": {"B": 80.0},
    "D_S07": {"B": 20.0}, "D_S10": {"B": 60.0}, "D_S06": {"B": 90.0},
    "D_S01": {"B": 80.0}, "D_S02": {"B": 80.0}, "D_S08": {"B": 80.0},
    "D_S09": {"B": 80.0},
}
print(nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=prom_by_group, by_group_plan=prom_by_group_plan, by_customer={}))
print(nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={g:0.0 for g in ["S1","S2","S3","D_S01","D_S02","D_S03","D_S04","D_S05","D_S06","D_S07","D_S08","D_S09","D_S10"]}, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={}))
print(nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}))

# Stop paid acquisition while retention/support is the bottleneck.
print(nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
    "content_marketing": {}, "social_media": {}, "search_ads": {}, "linkedin": {}, "referral_program": {}
}))

# Global spend: maintain strong ops floor, cut dev maintenance burn (targeted dev persists historically).
print(nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=50000.0, development=5000.0))

# Keep support focused on active issue creators, but trim from the prior emergency level.
ops_by_group = {
    "S2": 120000.0, "S3": 80000.0, "D_S07": 35000.0, "D_S10": 12000.0,
    "D_S09": 6000.0, "D_S02": 5000.0, "D_S04": 4000.0,
    "D_S08": 500.0, "S1": 500.0, "D_S05": 500.0, "D_S01": 250.0,
    "D_S03": 250.0, "D_S06": 250.0,
}
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(by_group=ops_by_group, by_plan={}, by_group_plan={}, by_customer={}))

# Reduced maintenance dev focused on groups that still generate nearly all MRR and reputation risk.
dev_by_group = {
    "S2": 12000.0, "S3": 12000.0, "D_S07": 5000.0, "D_S10": 2000.0,
    "D_S09": 1000.0, "D_S02": 1000.0, "D_S04": 1000.0,
}
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=dev_by_group))

# Usage is ~60M/day with no enterprise seats; tier6 (80M/day) should be adequate, monitor overload next week.
print(nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=6))

# Public support response to current S2 complaint.
try:
    msg = ("Rachel, unacceptable. We’ve moved healthcare/API tickets into a 24h incident queue, "
           "tied status updates to ticket IDs, and added named ETA owners + credits for delayed work. "
           "DM the ticket; I’ll route it today. Product quality must include support reliability.")
    print(nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=msg, reply_to_post_id=911))
except Exception as e:
    print('social post failed:', e)

# Enterprise offers: reply to every latest system/customer open thread, using group-specific margin-safe prices.
offers_by_group = {
    "E1": [["A", 14.0], ["B", 29.0], ["C", 99.0]],
    "E2": [["A", 39.0], ["B", 79.0], ["C", 249.0]],
    "E3": [["A", 39.0], ["B", 79.0], ["C", 149.0]],
    "D_E01": [["A", 49.0], ["B", 99.0], ["C", 199.0]],
    "D_E02": [["A", 29.0], ["B", 59.0], ["C", 149.0]],
    "D_E03": [["A", 69.0], ["B", 149.0], ["C", 249.0]],
    "D_E04": [["A", 59.0], ["B", 119.0], ["C", 219.0]],
    "D_E05": [["A", 49.0], ["B", 89.0], ["C", 179.0]],
    "D_E06": [["A", 29.0], ["B", 59.0], ["C", 129.0]],
    "D_E07": [["A", 59.0], ["B", 119.0], ["C", 209.0]],
    "D_E08": [["A", 59.0], ["B", 109.0], ["C", 199.0]],
    "D_E09": [["A", 39.0], ["B", 69.0], ["C", 139.0]],
    "D_E10": [["A", 25.0], ["B", 45.0], ["C", 99.0]],
}
def count_required():
    return nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
lt AS (SELECT e.* FROM enterprise_turns e JOIN latest l ON e.message_id=l.mid)
SELECT COUNT(*) n, COALESCE(SUM(seat_count),0) seats FROM lt WHERE sender IN ('system','customer')
""")['rows'][0]

print('enterprise required before', count_required())
processed = 0
for batch_no in range(1, 100):
    rows = nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
lt AS (SELECT e.* FROM enterprise_turns e JOIN latest l ON e.message_id=l.mid)
SELECT lt.customer_id AS cid, c.group_id AS gid
FROM lt JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=lt.customer_id
WHERE lt.sender IN ('system','customer')
ORDER BY lt.day, lt.customer_id
LIMIT 500
""")['rows']
    if not rows:
        break
    deals = [[int(r['cid']), offers_by_group.get(r['gid'], [["A", 49.0], ["B", 99.0], ["C", 199.0]])] for r in rows]
    res = nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=deals)
    ok = sum(1 for rr in res.get('results', []) if rr.get('success'))
    processed += ok
    print(f'batch {batch_no}: sent {ok}/{len(deals)}, processed_total={processed}')
    if ok == 0:
        print(json.dumps(res, indent=2)[:1000])
        break
print('enterprise required after', count_required())

# Final verification of key snapshot settings.
for row in nm.query("""
SELECT co.tool_name, co.setting_type, co.day, co.settings_json
FROM config_overrides co JOIN (SELECT setting_type, MAX(id) mid FROM config_overrides GROUP BY setting_type) x ON co.id=x.mid
ORDER BY co.setting_type
""")['rows']:
    print('latest_override', row)
print('capacity config', nm.query('SELECT * FROM config_history ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1')['rows'])

import novamind_api as nm, json
print('Week38 continuation day', nm.vars.current_day)
ops_by_group = {
    "S2": 120000.0, "S3": 80000.0, "D_S07": 35000.0, "D_S10": 12000.0,
    "D_S09": 6000.0, "D_S02": 5000.0, "D_S04": 4000.0,
    "D_S08": 500.0, "S1": 500.0, "D_S05": 500.0, "D_S01": 250.0,
    "D_S03": 250.0, "D_S06": 250.0,
}
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend=ops_by_group))

dev_by_group = {
    "S2": 12000.0, "S3": 12000.0, "D_S07": 5000.0, "D_S10": 2000.0,
    "D_S09": 1000.0, "D_S02": 1000.0, "D_S04": 1000.0,
}
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=dev_by_group))
print(nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=6))

try:
    msg = ("Rachel, unacceptable. We moved healthcare/API tickets into a 24h incident queue, "
           "tied status updates to ticket IDs, and added named ETA owners + credits for delayed work. "
           "DM the ticket; I’ll route it today. Quality must include support reliability.")
    print('msg_len', len(msg))
    print(nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=msg, reply_to_post_id=911))
except Exception as e:
    print('social post failed:', e)

offers_by_group = {
    "E1": [["A", 14.0], ["B", 29.0], ["C", 99.0]],
    "E2": [["A", 39.0], ["B", 79.0], ["C", 249.0]],
    "E3": [["A", 39.0], ["B", 79.0], ["C", 149.0]],
    "D_E01": [["A", 49.0], ["B", 99.0], ["C", 199.0]],
    "D_E02": [["A", 29.0], ["B", 59.0], ["C", 149.0]],
    "D_E03": [["A", 69.0], ["B", 149.0], ["C", 249.0]],
    "D_E04": [["A", 59.0], ["B", 119.0], ["C", 219.0]],
    "D_E05": [["A", 49.0], ["B", 89.0], ["C", 179.0]],
    "D_E06": [["A", 29.0], ["B", 59.0], ["C", 129.0]],
    "D_E07": [["A", 59.0], ["B", 119.0], ["C", 209.0]],
    "D_E08": [["A", 59.0], ["B", 109.0], ["C", 199.0]],
    "D_E09": [["A", 39.0], ["B", 69.0], ["C", 139.0]],
    "D_E10": [["A", 25.0], ["B", 45.0], ["C", 99.0]],
}

def count_required():
    return nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
lt AS (SELECT e.* FROM enterprise_turns e JOIN latest l ON e.message_id=l.mid)
SELECT COUNT(*) n, COALESCE(SUM(seat_count),0) seats FROM lt WHERE sender IN ('system','customer')
""")['rows'][0]

print('enterprise required before', count_required())
processed = 0
for batch_no in range(1, 100):
    rows = nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
lt AS (SELECT e.* FROM enterprise_turns e JOIN latest l ON e.message_id=l.mid)
SELECT lt.customer_id AS cid, c.group_id AS gid
FROM lt JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=lt.customer_id
WHERE lt.sender IN ('system','customer')
ORDER BY lt.day, lt.customer_id
LIMIT 500
""")['rows']
    if not rows:
        break
    deals = [[int(r['cid']), offers_by_group.get(r['gid'], [["A", 49.0], ["B", 99.0], ["C", 199.0]])] for r in rows]
    res = nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=deals)
    ok = sum(1 for rr in res.get('results', []) if rr.get('success'))
    processed += ok
    print(f'batch {batch_no}: sent {ok}/{len(deals)}, processed_total={processed}')
    if ok != len(deals):
        print(json.dumps(res, indent=2)[:2000])
    if ok == 0:
        break
print('enterprise required after', count_required())

for row in nm.query("""
SELECT co.tool_name, co.setting_type, co.day, co.settings_json
FROM config_overrides co JOIN (SELECT setting_type, MAX(id) mid FROM config_overrides GROUP BY setting_type) x ON co.id=x.mid
ORDER BY co.setting_type
""")['rows']:
    print('latest_override', row)
print('latest config_history', nm.query('SELECT * FROM config_history ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1')['rows'])

import novamind_api as nm, json, math, textwrap

def q(sql):
    r=nm.query(sql)
    rows=r.get('rows', [])
    if r.get('warning'): print('WARN', r['warning'])
    return rows

D=nm.vars.current_day
print('day', D)
try:
    print('cost_info')
    print(json.dumps(nm.infrastructure.get_cost_info(), indent=2)[:4000])
except Exception as e: print('cost_info err', e)

print('\nledger last 7 by cat')
for row in q(f"SELECT category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt FROM ledger WHERE day > {D-7} GROUP BY category ORDER BY amt DESC"): print(row)
print('\nledger last 14 by week/cat')
for row in q(f"SELECT CASE WHEN day>{D-7} THEN 'last7' ELSE 'prev7' END wk, category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt FROM ledger WHERE day>{D-14} GROUP BY wk, category ORDER BY wk, amt DESC"): print(row)
print('\nledger daily last 14')
for row in q(f"SELECT day, category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt FROM ledger WHERE day>{D-14} GROUP BY day, category ORDER BY day, category"): print(row)

print('\nactive subs by group/plan')
for row in q("""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats,
       ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan
ORDER BY mrr DESC
"""): print(row)

print('\nactive MRR by group')
for row in q("""
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id ORDER BY mrr DESC
"""): print(row)

print('\nnew subs last7 by group/plan')
for row in q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.start_day>{D-7} AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.status IN ('subscribed','cancelled')
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan ORDER BY n DESC
"""): print(row)

print('\ncancels last7 by group/plan/start cohort')
for row in q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, CASE WHEN s.start_day>={D-7} THEN 'new<=7d' WHEN s.start_day>={D-30} THEN '8-30d' WHEN s.start_day>={D-60} THEN '31-60d' ELSE 'old>60d' END cohort,
       COUNT(*) n, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) lost_mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day>{D-7} AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan, cohort ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 50
"""): print(row)

print('\nopen issues by group active-subs join')
for row in q("""
SELECT i.group_id, COUNT(*) open_issues, ROUND(AVG(i.days_open),2) avg_age, MAX(i.days_open) max_age,
       SUM(CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) open_active
FROM issues i LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=i.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY i.group_id ORDER BY open_issues DESC
"""): print(row)
print('\nopen active issue ages')
for row in q("""
SELECT i.group_id, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(AVG(i.days_open),2) avg_age, MAX(i.days_open) max_age
FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=i.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY i.group_id ORDER BY n DESC
"""): print(row)
print('\nissues opened/resolved last7 by group')
for row in q(f"""
SELECT group_id,
 SUM(CASE WHEN open_day>{D-7} THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) opened7,
 SUM(CASE WHEN resolved_day>{D-7} THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) resolved7,
 SUM(CASE WHEN status='open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) open_now
FROM issues GROUP BY group_id ORDER BY open_now DESC
"""): print(row)

print('\nenterprise active subs/seats by group')
for row in q("""
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='large'
GROUP BY c.group_id ORDER BY seats DESC
"""): print(row)

print('\nenterprise leads current open latest sender counts')
for row in q("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id
), lt AS (
 SELECT e.customer_id, e.sender, e.thread_type, e.day, e.seat_count
 FROM enterprise_turns e JOIN latest l ON e.message_id=l.mid
)
SELECT sender, thread_type, COUNT(*) threads, SUM(seat_count) seats, MIN(day) min_day, MAX(day) max_day FROM lt GROUP BY sender, thread_type ORDER BY threads DESC
"""): print(row)
print('\nrequired enterprise replies count')
for row in q("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
lt AS (SELECT e.* FROM enterprise_turns e JOIN latest l ON e.message_id=l.mid)
SELECT COUNT(*) n, SUM(seat_count) seats, MIN(day) min_day, MAX(day) max_day
FROM lt WHERE sender IN ('system','customer')
"""): print(row)

print('\nrecent config_history')
for row in q("SELECT * FROM config_history ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 5"): print(row)
print('\nlatest config overrides')
for row in q("""
SELECT co.tool_name, co.setting_type, co.day, co.settings_json
FROM config_overrides co JOIN (
  SELECT setting_type, MAX(id) mid FROM config_overrides GROUP BY setting_type
) x ON co.id=x.mid
ORDER BY co.setting_type
"""): print(row)

print('\nresearch projects')
try: print(json.dumps(nm.research.list_research_projects(), indent=2)[:5000])
except Exception as e: print('research err', e)
print('\nsocial posts')
try:
    posts=nm.analytics.get_social_posts(days=7, limit=80)
    print(json.dumps(posts, indent=2)[:12000])
except Exception as e: print('social err', e)

import novamind_api as nm
D=nm.vars.current_day
q=lambda sql: nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])
print('Current day',D,'mod',D%30)
print('Active monthly MRR by billing mod')
for r in q('''
SELECT billing_day_mod30 mod, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(effective_price*seat_count),2) mrr
FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed' GROUP BY billing_day_mod30 ORDER BY billing_day_mod30
'''): print(r)
print('Next 14 days expected billed active MRR by calendar day')
for i in range(1,15):
    mod=(D+i)%30
    rows=q(f"SELECT COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(effective_price*seat_count),2) mrr FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed' AND billing_day_mod30={mod}")
    print(D+i, 'mod',mod, rows[0])
# Historical subscription payments by day/mod last60
print('historical sub by day last60')
for r in q(f"SELECT day, day%30 mod, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) sub FROM ledger WHERE category='subscription_payment' AND day>{D-60} GROUP BY day ORDER BY day"): print(r)

import novamind_api as nm, json, re
D=nm.vars.current_day
q=lambda sql: nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])
print('lead groups required')
for r in q('''
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
lt AS (SELECT e.* FROM enterprise_turns e JOIN latest l ON e.message_id=l.mid)
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) leads, SUM(lt.seat_count) seats, ROUND(AVG(lt.seat_count),1) avg_seats, MIN(lt.seat_count) min_seats, MAX(lt.seat_count) max_seats
FROM lt JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=lt.customer_id
WHERE lt.sender IN ('system','customer')
GROUP BY c.group_id ORDER BY seats DESC
'''): print(r)
print('\nsample latest required messages')
for r in q('''
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
lt AS (SELECT e.* FROM enterprise_turns e JOIN latest l ON e.message_id=l.mid)
SELECT lt.customer_id, c.group_id, lt.seat_count, SUBSTR(lt.message_text,1,300) msg
FROM lt JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=lt.customer_id
WHERE lt.sender IN ('system','customer')
ORDER BY lt.seat_count DESC LIMIT 20
'''): print(r)
print('\ninsights enterprise')
for gid in ['E1','E2','E3']+[f'D_E{i:02d}' for i in range(1,11)]:
    try:
        ins=nm.market.get_group_insights(group_id=gid)
        est=ins.get('estimates',{})
        print(gid, ins.get('group_name'), 'info', ins.get('info_level'), 'wtp', est.get('willingness_to_pay'), 'usage', est.get('usage_volume'), 'qmin', est.get('quality_floor_q_min'), 'seats', est.get('seat_range'), 'rounds', est.get('negotiation_rounds'), 'pace', est.get('negotiation_pace_days'))
    except Exception as e: print(gid, 'err', e)

import novamind_api as nm
D=nm.vars.current_day
q=lambda sql: nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])
mods=','.join(str((D+i)%30) for i in range(1,8))
print('mods',mods)
for r in q(f'''
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan,
 CASE WHEN s.start_day>={D-30} THEN 'lt30' WHEN s.start_day>={D-60} THEN '31-60' ELSE 'old' END cohort,
 COUNT(*) n, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({mods})
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,cohort ORDER BY mrr DESC LIMIT 80
'''): print(r)
print('nextweek active MRR total by cohort')
for r in q(f'''
SELECT CASE WHEN s.start_day>={D-30} THEN 'lt30' WHEN s.start_day>={D-60} THEN '31-60' ELSE 'old' END cohort,
 COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({mods})
GROUP BY cohort ORDER BY mrr DESC
'''): print(r)

import novamind_api as nm, json
q=lambda sql: nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])
for r in q('''
SELECT c.group_id, e.day, e.customer_id, e.offer_json
FROM enterprise_turns e JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=e.customer_id
WHERE e.sender='agent' AND e.day=259
LIMIT 20
'''): print(r)
print('agent offers by day counts')
for r in q('''SELECT day, COUNT(*) n FROM enterprise_turns WHERE sender='agent' GROUP BY day ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 10'''): print(r)
